frozen_components: Qwen3-VL-8B backbone (implicit; fine-tuned via RL, not pre-trained frozen)
architecture.summary: OmniVerifier-M1 is a multimodal verifier built on Qwen3-VL-8B that performs visual outcome verification and generates structured rationales (bounding boxes and semantic edit instructions). During training, it is optimized via reinforcement learning with two decoupled objectives: an accuracy reward for binary True/False judgments, and a meta-verification reward (rule-based IoU) for localization quality. The system outputs are consumed by M1-TTS, an agentic generation loop where a unified multimodal model (UMM) receives the verifier's spatial (bboxes) and semantic (atomic edit actions) signals to iteratively refine generated images until verification succeeds or max iterations elapse.

biblio.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration

claims.statement: Symbolic verifier outputs (e.g., bounding boxes) outperform textual explanations as meta-verification rationales, enabling efficient rule-based reinforcement learning rewards while avoiding reliance on model-based rewards from auxiliary judge models.
Symbolic bounding box meta-verification achieves comparable performance to textual explanations on ViVerBench while reducing per-sample reward computation time from 20.2 ms to 0.021 ms.
Symbolic bounding box meta-verification reduces per-card GPU memory usage from 56.9 GB to 48.6 GB compared to textual explanations during training on OmniVerifier-7B.

Decoupling reinforcement learning objectives for binary judgment and meta-verification substantially outperforms joint reward optimization, due to intrinsic differences in output structure and learning dynamics.
OmniVerifier-7B with decoupled training achieves 0.668 on ViVerBench, outperforming joint training at 0.661 and baseline OmniVerifier-7B at 0.650.
Qwen3-VL-8B with decoupled training achieves 0.866 on RefCOCO overall, outperforming joint training at 0.847 and baseline Qwen3-VL-8B at 0.831.
M1-TTS with OmniVerifier-M1 and GPT-Image-1.5 achieves 0.88 on WISE overall and 0.800 on T2I-CoreBench overall, outperforming Qwen3-VL-8B+GPT-Image-1.5 baseline at 0.86 and 0.787 respectively.

Theorem 5.3 shows that in joint RL training, meta-verification gradients are multiplicatively gated by the verifier's accuracy, suppressing gradient magnitude and introducing variance; this effect is eliminated in decoupled training.
